title: Using "Column" in Settings to Get Column Name
excerpt:
Hi, 
How do I get the column name from the settings when using the “Column” setting in a board view? At first glance, the column input in the settings is exactly what I wanted: to choose a column in a board. But when you…

posts.author: jBoetticher (Jeremy Boetticher)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
How do I get the column name from the settings when using the “Column” setting in a board view? At first glance, the column input in the settings is exactly what I wanted: to choose a column in a board. But when you look at what comes out, all you get is a boolean. How do I get identifying information about the column from the settings so that I can match it up with the board data?

Screen Shot 2022-07-23 at 7.09.17 PM1920×1022 90.2 KB

Regards,
Jeremy

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @jBoetticher!

The id of the column is in the response you get, before the value of the boolean.

In your case, the column ids would be “numbers” and “people”.

I hope that helps!

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: jBoetticher (Jeremy Boetticher)
posts.cookedHtml:
Unfortunately the column selection feature stopped working in entirety. It no longer shows my columns, and trying to create a column throws an error in the console.

Screen Shot 2022-07-31 at 12.34.39 PM1920×1242 224 KB

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @jBoetticher!

Can you please send an email to appsupport@monday.com with the description of the issue, all the screenshots you think are relevant and the script you are using so we can take a closer look?

Looking forward to hearing from you

Cheers,
Matias
